assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements.
An intragroup dividend has given rise to a recognised receivable/payable denominated in a foreign currency or creating foreign exchange exposure.
The issue is the IFRS accounting model relevant to designating that foreign exchange exposure in a documented hedging relationship.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedging relationships as a distinct model overriding the default fair value gain or loss presentation, and fair value hedge accounting is one of the top-level hedge accounting models relevant to recognised items.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IAS 21 identify cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposures, including highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9, IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 treat hedges of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign currency translation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the default accounting model for exchange differences on foreign currency monetary items, including intragroup monetary balances in consolidated financial statements.
